div.p: C Y R O G R A P H

p.seg: This is the agreement made between the lord abbot, W., and the convent of Holyrood in Edinburgh on the one side and Walter the Master, and the brethren of Torphichen on the other side, concerning the dispute which arose between them and was aired for a long while before judges delegate, over the teinds and revenues from Ogilface, that the aforesaid abbot and convent, with the consent and authority of the judges delegate, in the presence of the lord William del Bois, chancellor of the lord king, and other judicious men, for the sake of good peace, granted to the aforesaid brethen all the teinds and ecclesiastical revenues that they themselves used to have and were accustomed to receive from the land of Ogilface. The aforesaid brethren, however, shall hold and in perpetuity possess the aforenamed teinds freely and quietly, fully and honourably, by rendering thereupon to the abbot and convent of Holyrood in Edinburgh each year four merks of silver, two at Pentecost and two at the feast of Saint Martin.

In order that this agreement remain sure and unbroken in perpetuity the seals of the judges delegate of the aforesaid case and the chapter of the convent of St Andrews and the chapter of the hospital of London [Clerkenwell?] are set to the present document in testimony and confirmation of this agreement lest it could be revoked as being invalid.
